text:
In the quiet desert town of Whispering Pines, there lived a wise old falcon named Frodo. Frodo lived alone, in a nest perched atop a gnarled oak tree. Every morning, he floated on the breeze to his favorite spot overlooking the golden sunrises.

One day, while catching a mouse, Frodo felt something unusual tickling his feathers. Peeking between his wings, he saw a small, sparkling being. Instantly curious, Frodo embarked on a quest to find this mysterious little thing. After several exhausting flights, he finally stumbled upon a shimmering pool that hosted hundreds of other sparkling entities.

These were the Glitterflies—the most elusive faeries of Whispering Pines. They weren't frightened; their wings danced in the light. Frodo approached them tentatively.

"My wings are heavy," he mused. "And my heart's quick with anticipation."

They giggled softly. One fluttered closer and whispered into Frodo's ear.
